The Ultimate eSIM Guide

In today’s world, traveling without internet access isn’t just inconvenient—it’s nearly impossible. Whether you’re 🗺️ navigating unfamiliar streets, 🛫 booking last-minute tickets, or 💬 staying connected with loved ones, being online is essential. But relying on your home network’s roaming services is almost always a bad idea, often leading to outrageous fees. A far smarter and more cost-effective solution is to either buy a local physical SIM card at your destination or use an eSIM. 📲 Thanks to advances in mobile technology, eSIMs have become the new standard—so much so that many of today’s smartphones no longer support physical SIM cards at all. This guide will help you compare providers and choose the best eSIM for your needs. 🌍 My country-specific guides also offer tailored advice depending on your destination and trip duration.
💡 Note: This guide is entirely unbiased. I don’t earn money or receive sponsorships for any recommendations—no affiliate links, no paid promos.

eSIM vs. SIM Card – What’s the Best Option for Travelers?

So, which option is better? The answer depends on several factors—the most important being your destination and the length of your trip. Before we dive into how to choose the best eSIM at the best price, let’s take a step back and compare eSIMs and physical SIM cards in more detail. To help you decide, I’ve created a simple side-by-side comparison below.
Feature eSIM Physical Sim
Availability & Setup
✅ Instant download and activation. Easy online setup via app, link or QR code.
❌ Must purchase locally at the destination. Requires visiting a vendor, waiting in line, and dealing with local language barriers.
Convenience
✅ No physical card required, fully digital.
❌ Requires swapping SIM cards and keeping track of a physical chip.
Cost for Short Trips
✅ Often cheaper for short trips due to flexible plans. (Depends on the Destination)
❌ Typically less cost-efficient for short trips. (Depends on the Destination)
Cost for Long Trips
❌ Can be pricier due to ongoing data plan fees.(Depends on the Destination)
✅ Often cheaper for long stays with local plans. (Depends on the Destination)
Provider Options
✅ Easily compare and purchase plans from multiple providers online, offering transparency.
❌ Less number of providers and inconsistent pricing changing from store to store.
Hotspot Sharing
❌ Limited with some providers, depending on the plan.
✅ Fully functional and reliable for most SIMs.
Eco-Friendliness
✅ No plastic waste, contributing to a greener planet.
❌ Creates plastic waste and packaging.
Customer Support: Installation
❌ May involve navigating app-based activation, which can be tricky for some users. Support is remote, via app or email.
✅ Installation is done in-store by employees, ensuring it works before you leave.
Customer Support: In Use
✅ Remote support via app, email, or chat is accessible globally and often multilingual.
❌ Finding support later can be difficult, especially in foreign countries with language barriers.
From the comparison, eSIMs often emerge as the better option for most travelers. However, the choice still depends on your 🌍 destination and 🕒 trip duration—there are cases where a physical SIM might be more practical. The real challenge is finding the best eSIM provider at the lowest price 💸. This guide will walk you through that process, covering the benefits of eSIMs, setup tips, and how to choose the right plan for your trip. Based on my personal experience testing eSIMs and physical SIMs across multiple countries 🌐, I’ve found eSIMs to be more convenient and cost-effective in most situations.

How to Choose the Best eSIM for Your Trip

Choosing the right eSIM depends on three key factors: 🌍 Your destination 🕒 The duration of your trip 📶 The amount of data you need Each of these elements plays a crucial role in determining which provider and package will best suit your travel needs.

Step 1: How to Check if Your Phone is Compatible with eSIM (iPhone & Android Guide)

📱 Before you purchase an eSIM, it’s important to make sure your device is compatible with eSIM technology. While most modern smartphones do support eSIMs, there are a few quick checks you should perform to avoid any issues. 🔍 Here’s how you can check compatibility on both iPhone and Android devices:
🍏 On iPhone:
  1. Open the Settings app.
  2. Go to General > About.
  3. Look for the field labeled EID: • If you see an EID number, ✅ your iPhone supports eSIMs. • If the field is missing, ❌ your iPhone does not support eSIMs.
🤖 On Android:
  1. Open the Settings app.
  2. Navigate to About Phone or About Device (this may vary by model).
  3. Look for EID or a second IMEI number: • If either is present, ✅ your device supports eSIMs. • If neither is present, ❌ your device does not support eSIMs.
🔓 Even if your device supports eSIMs, it must be unlocked to use eSIMs from different providers. Here’s how to check:
🍏 On iPhone:
  1. Go to Settings > General > About.
  2. Scroll to find the field labeled Carrier Lock or Network Provider Lock: • “No SIM Restrictions” ✅ — Your phone is unlocked and can use eSIMs from any provider. • “[Carrier Name] Only” 🔒 — Your phone is locked to a specific carrier, and you’ll need to contact them to unlock it.
🤖 On Android:
  1. Open Settings > Connections > Mobile Networks > Network Unlock (or a similar option, depending on your device).
  2. Check if the phone shows an option to “Request Unlock” or indicates that it’s locked: • 🔒 If locked, contact your carrier to request an unlock. • ✅ If unlocked, you’re free to use eSIMs from any provider.

Step 2: How to Compare eSIM Options to Find the Best One for Your Next Trip?

🌐 The second—and most important—step is to compare eSIM providers and their packages. Fortunately, there’s a great tool to help with this: eSIMDB. This website lets you easily compare different eSIM providers and plans tailored to your destination and travel needs. ➡️ Head over to eSIMDB and type your destination country in the search bar. For example, if you’re traveling to Japan, enter “Japan”—you’ll see it appear below the search field. Click on it to view relevant plans. 🧭 Follow along—I’ll walk you through how to use eSIMDB effectively to optimize your search.

Once the country page loads, you’ll see a list of eSIM plans. Follow these steps to refine your search:

🔧 Want to get the most out of eSIMDB? Here’s what to do:

✅ Start by selecting the “Cheapest” option to sort all available plans from lowest to highest in total cost.

💸 Don’t forget to turn on the “Apply discounts” toggle. It’s a quick way to unlock special prices and hidden deals.

💱 Prefer to see prices in your local currency? Just click the currency icon and choose your preferred one—it makes comparisons much easier.

🎯 Use the “Filters” button to fine-tune your results. You can filter by data allowance, validity period, or even specific providers.

🛠️ In the filter section, here’s what I recommend doing:

📊 Start by setting a minimum data limit. For example, if you know you’ll be using navigation and social media a lot, try setting it to at least 10GB.

📅 Next, define the minimum trip duration. This helps eliminate ultra-short plans that won’t be useful for your journey—something like 10 days is a good starting point.

🚫 Then, toggle on “Hide daily data cap plans”. Trust me, you don’t want a plan that slows you down after 500MB each day.

✅ Once everything looks good, just hit “OK” and enjoy your filtered list of smart choices.

📄 Once you’ve applied your filters, you’ll see a more targeted list of eSIM plans.

🔍 Pay attention to the top 3–10 results—they typically include the best value for your money.

💡 For instance, in one search I did, eSIMplus offered a plan for only $5.67 USD, which was much cheaper than most others listed.

Note down the top three options that match your needs. The next step involves checking customer reviews to ensure the provider’s reliability and service quality.

Step 3: How to Use Trustpilot to Compare eSIM Providers for Your Destination?

🧾 After narrowing down your options on eSIMDB to your top 2–3 providers, the next key step is to evaluate the customer experience

⭐ For this, I recommend using Trustpilot — a trustworthy, independent platform with real customer reviews for most eSIM companies

  • ✅ Start with the overall rating — A score above 4.0 stars usually indicates reliable service
  • 🌍 Check country-specific reviews — Look for feedback related to your destination, since providers may work with different local networks in each country
  • 🔍 Use filters and keywords — Search for reviews mentioning things like connection quality, customer support, or setup issues in your destination country

🔎 Up next: How to actually use Trustpilot to search effectively 👇

🔎 1. Identify the Actual Company Name: Sometimes, the company name listed on eSIMDB differs from its official business name. To find the correct company name for review purposes: ➡️ Open the selected provider’s listing on eSIMDB. ➡️ Click the “Official Website” button. 📌 For example: The provider eSIMplus actually corresponds to the company T-sim.hk.

🔍 2. Search on Trustpilot

Go to Trustpilot and enter the company name in the search bar. For example

📉 T-sim.hk (eSIMplus) — Has 1 review with a 3.7 rating. While limited reviews might seem risky, my personal experience in Japan suggests it can still be reliable

✅ Airdatalink — Holds a solid 4.2 rating from multiple reviews—generally a safe choice

🏆 Mobimatter — With a 4.2 rating and over 17,000 reviews, this provider is highly reputable and trusted

🧠 3. Refine Your Decision Based on Reviews

If you’re still unsure, try to find reviews that mention your specific destination

🔎 Use Trustpilot’s filter or search options to look for country-specific experiences

🌏 For example, enter “Japan” in the search or review filter to see feedback from travelers who used the service there

🧐 4. Understanding Bad Reviews

Not all bad reviews mean the service is poor. Many negative experiences come from avoidable issues, such as

❗ Misreading the installation guide — eSIM setup can be a bit tricky if the instructions aren’t followed exactly

📱 Phone compatibility issues — some users leave bad reviews without checking if their device supports eSIMs in the first place

🧩 That’s why it’s important to read through the reviews carefully and look for patterns. A few isolated bad reviews don’t necessarily mean the provider is unreliable

Final Decision:
Providers with a rating above 4.5 are generally safe bets. However, if you prefer to minimize risks, opt for the provider with the highest ratings and relevant positive reviews for your destination.

Final Words

🌍 Choosing the right eSIM for your travels can make all the difference in staying connected without stress. While this guide gives you the tools to make informed decisions, the journey doesn’t end here

🧳 In my destination-specific guides, I share detailed reviews and personal experiences with various eSIM providers I’ve used across different countries

📶 From connection quality to ease of setup and customer support, you’ll find insights tailored to each destination—helping you make even more confident choices

✨ Stay tuned as I continue testing and sharing my findings to make your travel planning easier, smarter, and more reliable

🛫 Safe travels, and happy exploring!
